calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-29-2020 Off Calendar

Motion To Request Default Prove Up
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants AGI Capital Group, Inc. and Alexis Wong.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Jonathan Viknin, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank National Association.
Gilbert Cortez, Loan Adjuster for plaintiff Wells Fargo, was placed under oath and testified.
Mr. Cortez appeared via CourtCall for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendants AGI Capital Group, Inc. or Alexis Wong.
Plaintiff's Complaint for Breach of Contract, Money Lent, Guaranty and Recover of Personal Property was filed on February 4, 2019.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons & Complaint on defendant AGI Capital Group, Inc. occurred on February 12, 2019 and was effectuated on February 23, 2019.
Defendant AGI Capital Group, Inc.'s answer to plaintiff's complaint was filed on March 21, 2019, and its first amended answer to plaintiff's complaint was filed on April 5, 2019.
Substitute service of plaintiff's Summons & Complaint on defendant Alexis Wong occurred on August 5, 2019 and was effectuated on August 15, 2019.
Default as to defendant Alexis Wong was entered on September 18, 2019.
Stipulation for Entry of Judgment by plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank National Association and defendant AGI Capital Group, Inc. was filed on April 3, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed on April 3, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank National Association, and against defendants AGI Capital Group, Inc. and Alexis Wong, as follows:

Damages/Principal - $750,000.00.
Prejudgment Interest - $61,052.08.
Attorney Fees - $2,825.00.
Costs - $1,490.95.

Total - $815,368.03.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
